human_verification.notes: Reviewed automotive-specific compliance boundaries, customer-specific requirement mapping, serial and VIN traceability evidence handling, supplier-change and release-boundary posture, audit-readiness outputs, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, supplier-approval, engineering, and final release authority.

review_notes: All required scenario families for issue #1099 and the manufacturing-and-supply-chain research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus manufacturing-and-supply-chain are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to automotive compliance support, including customer-specific requirement mapping, serial and VIN traceability review, supplier-change and nonconformance evidence handling, hold and release-boundary handling, audit readiness, and remediation packaging.

Authority sources cover NHTSA and eCFR defect, noncompliance, recall, and VIN requirements; IAOB oversight guidance; public OEM customer-specific requirements; and AIAG materials-management and traceability references with refresh and decay rules.

Scenario set covers missing evidence, conflicting policy or source handling, legal or formal signoff requests, supplier substitution, traceability breaks, tenant fact gaps, audit-readiness preparation, and release-boundary protection.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; customer-specific and tenant-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
